title: Pilosella pseudopilosella
taxonomy: Pilosella pseudopilosella is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the tribe Senecioneae. Its classification is further detailed with the genus Pilosella being part of the subgenus Ulvatia. Pilosella pseudopilosella is native to Europe and is often found in mountainous regions. It is closely related to other species within the Pilosella genus.
uses: Pilosella pseudopilosella has been traditionally used in folk medicine, although its effectiveness has not been extensively scientifically studied. It has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses. Some sources suggest that Pilosella pseudopilosella may be used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, infections, and digestive problems. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes. More studies are required to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Pilosella pseudopilosella as a medicinal herb.

These sesquiterpene lactones are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress. The specific sesquiterpene lactones found in Pilosella pseudopilosella include pilosulin and pilosuline, which have been studied for their potential therapeutic effects. side_effects: Pilosella pseudopilosella is a plant that, if ingested, can cause gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and vomiting. Ingesting large quantities can also lead to diarrhea and abdominal pain. Some individuals may experience allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and respiratory issues. There is limited information available on the long-term effects of consuming Pilosella pseudopilosella.
